displayName: Payer System Disposition (Da Vinci PAS / X12 278)
description: The payer Utilization-Management system's auto-adjudication disposition, as rendered by the external system of record (Da Vinci PAS $submit -> ClaimResponse, carrying the X12 005010/306 review action code). Notarize-only: AGLedger NOTARIZES the disposition the SoR rendered; it does not render it. This is the SoR-seam capture — the immutable record of what the payer system decided, when, and on what basis. A1 (certified) and A3 (not-required) are terminal here (clean auto-adjudication, no human gate). A2 (denied) and A4 (pended) are contested and escalate to a medical director via halcyon-pa-determination-v1 — because no compliant payer lets AI render an autonomous denial.

paRequestRef.description: recordId of the halcyon-pa-request-v1 this disposition answers.

payerDisposition.description: X12 005010/306 review action: A1 certified in total | A2 not certified (denied) | A3 not required | A4 pended (more info) | A6 modified.

reviewActionDisplay.description: Human-readable display from the ClaimResponse (e.g. 'Not Certified').

x12System.description: The code system URI, e.g. https://codesystem.x12.org/005010/306.

claimResponseId.description: FHIR ClaimResponse.id from the PAS response — the cross-reference into the payer SoR.

reasonCode.description: Disposition reason / denial reason code where the SoR supplies one.

autoAdjudicated.description: true when the SoR rendered this without a human (the auto-adjudication layer); the contested path then routes to a medical director.
